This company was registered in the Transvaal November 9th, 1895, to acquire the assets of the East Rand Synd., Ltd., formed in January, 1893, comprising 275 claims, south of the Witwatersrand Knight s Mine, on farm Driefontein, Boksbnrg, Eastern Witwatersrand, Transvaal. The consideration was £286,500, in fully-paid shares ; 65,000 shares were offered to shareholders of the syndicate at £2 10s., the issue being guaranteed by S. Neumann 4 Co. and H. Eckstein & Co. The company subsequently purchased 10 claims and a water-right on farm Klippoortje for £4,720 cash, making 285 claims now held. Crushing commenced in November, 1902, with 60 hired stamps, and stopped in June, 1904 ; the company has ereoted its own 220-stamp mill with cyanide and slimes plant, and crushing commenced in December, 1904. During 1906, 835,880 tons produced Bold value £561,407 or 33s. 51d. per ton, at a cost of 20s. 9 ld. and a profit of 12s. 8d. per ton; during 1907, 386,110 tons produced gold value £667,967 and a profit of £289,461. For crushings set table at commencement of volume. Payable ore reserves at December, 1906, 787,052 tons at September 30th, 1907, 895,430 tons. The authorised capital is £550,000, in 550,000 shares of £1 each; 500,000 shares are issued and fully paid. The capital was increased from £400,000 to £450,000 . 503 WITWATEBSRAND DBEP. in 1898, and to present amount in 1902, when 100,000 shares were offered to shareholders at £4 per share ; the issue was guaranteed at £3 15s. £100,000 five and a half per cent, debentures in bonds of £10, £50, and £100 were offered to shareholders in April, 1898, at par und allotted, and a further £100,000 were issued in March, 1899 ; interest is payable January 1st and July 1st, and the bonds are redeemable at par in 15 aunual drawings, first issue commencing January, 1901, and second January, 1902, for repay men July 1st following, or at 105 per cent, at any time upon six months notice from the company, or by purchasing and cancelling bonds as part of any annual drawing. £74,000 in all are now outstanding. Dividends declared paid about seven weeks after declaration : for 1906, 9s. 6d. declared, 4s. per share March 31st, and 5s. lid. December 31st, 1906; for 1907, 8s. Od., declared 4s. Jane 25th, 1907, and 4s. 6d. December 19th, 1907. The accounts for year ended December 31st, 1905, submitted In Johannesburg February 21st, 1906, showed gold receipts £415,020 and credit balance increased from £65,004 to £85,119, after charging £94,928 capital expenditure. The accounts to December 31st, 1906, submitted in Johannesburg February 20th, 1907, showed gold receipt £561,407 and a profit of 212,715, making with balance forward, reserve gold and sundry revenue, a sum of £316,538 ; dividends absorbed £237,500, expended on capital account £39,522, debenture redemption, interest, &t ., £20,896, leaving £18,620 carried forward. A share register is kept in London. Transfer form, common ; fee, 2s. 6d. per transfer of 100 shares or part thereof. This company was registered in the Transvaal on the 29th November, 1886, to acquire the farm Driefontein, Eastern Witwatersrand, 10,355 acres in extent, with a mynpacht of 300 claims. The vendors received £45,000 in cash and £150,000 in shares. In January, 1889, the company sold 36 claims for £210,000 in cash and £15,000 in shares of the Balmoral Main Reef Gold Mining Co., Ltd. In May, 1893, 12 claims adjoining the Glencairn property, with the corresponding 48 dip claims, making 60 in all, were sold to Barnato Bros, for £60,000. The company now owns Mynpacht No. 320, 296 morgen in extent, 78 prospecting and two diggers claims and 4,780 morgen freehold on farm Drietfontein, No. 148, Boksburg district, Transvaal, and also derives a revenue of about £11,000 per annum from a residential estate of 4,892 acres, situate on the said farm to the north of the main reef series. Crushing commenced in January, 1888, with a 50-stamp mill, and further 50 stamps were afterwards added, but crushing was suspended in 1891 and the mine has since been thoroughly developed and a new 120-stamp mill and cyanide plant erected, crushing being resumed in 1896. A further 100 stamps making 220 and slimes plant have been added and are now at work. During 1906, 374,000 tons yielded gold value£501,044 or 26s. 9-5d. per ton, at a cost of 19s. -ld. and a profit of 7s. 5 3d. per ton ; during 1907. 402,100 tons yielded nold value £538,272 ; estimated working profit £194,150. The capital, formerly £210,000 in £10 shares, was increased and altered in 1888 to £250,000 in £1 shares ; increased in 1896, to £325,000, 62,500 shares being issued at £4 ; in 1897, 12,817 shares were sold at £4 12s. 3d. average ; in 1899, capital was increased to £375,000, and 27,083 shares issued to shareholders at £6 ; the remaining 22,917 were issued in 1902 at £7 10s., the Johannesburg Consolidated Investment Co., Ltd., being allotted 3,357 at that price and guaranteeing the remaining 19,560, which were offered to shareholders. In June, 1904, the capital was increased to present amount and 37,500 shares were offered to shareholders at £5 10s. each, the issue being guaranteed ; the remaining 12,500 were sold at £5 each in May, 1905. Tnrs company was first registered in the Transvaal September 8th, 1887, as the Bantjes Reef Gold Mining Co., L td., to acquire 104 gold-miningelaims on the Farms Vogelstruisfontein and Langlaagtc, WitwaterBrand, Transvaal The vendors received £70,000 in fully-paid shares. In December, 1888, 16 claims on the Langlaagte Farm, with adjoining inynpacht 33 morgen, were sold for £60,000 cash. In 1893 six south claims were purchased for £1,150. On September 9th, 1895, the company was reconstructed in the Transvaal under the present title, various adjoin ing properties being acquired for 196,250 shares . A further 25 claims were after wards acquired for 16,700 shares. Early in 1896, the company purchased for £76,000 cash the freehold of 3,148 acres, comprising about half of the farm Vogel struisfontein known as Van der Hoven s portion, including the part on which the company s mining claims arc situated, also including the Old and New Florida Township and the Florida Lake water-right. Mining area now held, 813 claims, also four water-rights. Three shafts are being sunk, and at September 30th, 1909, the east incline shaft has been sunk to a depth of 1,650 feet, the central shaft to 810 feet and the west shaft to C48 feet ; ore reserves at September 80th, 1909, 580,707 tons, value 6 4dwts. per ton. A 70-stamp battery tube mill, and cyanide and slimes plant are being erected, and crushing is expected to commence about Maroh, 1910. Provided the development operations continue satisfactory the estimated life will exceed 40 years treating 120,000 tons yearly. A revenue is derived from claim licences on the freehold and from stands in the Old Florida Township j the New portion is about to be dealt with. The authorised capital is £322,123, in 322,123 shares of £1 each; 306031 shares are issued and fully paid. Under the chome of reconstruction in 1895, five fully-paid new shares were given for every four old shares, with the right to subscribe for one new share at 50s. The guarantors took 36,250 shares at 50s., in return for an option over 60,000 reserve shares at 60s. This option was exorcised in September, 1896. In August, 1908, the capital, formerly £435,000, was reduced to £108,588 by cancelling 650 reserve shares and issuing 1 share for every 4 shares held : it was then increased to £295.200 and 140,000 shares offered pro rata to shareholders at par, being 8 for every £5 old hares held ; the issue was guaranteed ; subscribers received an option to November 11th, 1910, at 22 . 6d. per share over the same number of shares subscribed for, such rights being covered by a separate nominative transferable certificate ; the guarantors have the right to take up any shares at 22b. 6d. not taken up by shareholders under the option. The capital was increased to present amount in November, 1909. In May, 1909, 30,000 shares were taken up by H. Eckstein & Co., at 75s. each. The accounts to December 81st, 1908, submitted in Johannesburg Ma roh 3rd . 193 BANTJES CONSOLIDATED-oontiuued. 1909, showed cash £60,297, debtors £598 ; oreditors £3,488. In 1903, 32 adjoining claims were acquired and a further 10 in 1904. Total claims now held, 69 ; also 53 claims and water rights in Heidelberg distriot ; 52,0 0 fully paid shares in Great Oriental Gold Mines, Ltd. are also held. Development work is at present upended. This company was registered July 18tb, 1909, to acquire as from June 30th, 1909, a mining lease granted by the Egyptian Government of the Barramia Mining property, area 50 acres, with 5-Btamp mill and other machinery, situate between the Nile and the Red Sea, Upper Xgypt and about 63 miles from Edfou. The consideration was £34,350 payable £5,000 cash and 10,800 fully paid preference and 112,000 fully paid ordinary shares. A mineral prospecting license over 150 acres surrounding the property has also been acquired from the Government. The company was promoted by the vendors, the Egypt and Sudan Mining Syndicates, Ltd. Crushing was commenced by the vendors in June, 1907, the yield to June, 1909, being £17,190 from 1,460 tons crushed. Crushing is in progress. The authorised capital is £55,000, in 216,000 preference shares of 2s. 6d. each, and 112,000 ordinary shares of 6s. each ; all the shares are issued, 10,800 prefer ence and all the ordinary credited as fully paid, and 205,200 preference having 2s. paid, the remaining 6d. being payable May 2nd, 1910. The preference shares are entitled to a 10 per cent, non-cumulative dividend, after which they participate with the ordinary shares in any balance of profit ; in liquida tion the preference shares have priority in repayment of the capital. In July, 1909, shareholders in the vendor syndicate and the Suakin Mining Synd., Ltd., were invited to subscribe for 205,200 preference shares, payable 6d. on application, 6d. on allotment, and the balance in calls of 6d. on November 1st, 1909, February 1st, 1910, and May 2nd, 1910. The company is work ing surface deposits of low grade ore, and has also tree plantations ; prospecting for quartz reefs is also proceeding.
